plan: Create `src/scoring/permission-debt-score.ts` with: (a) a `PermissionWeight` record mapping each of the 11 standard permissions to a risk weight (e.g., camera/microphone/geolocation weighted higher than accelerometer/gyroscope), (b) a pure function `calculatePermissionDebtScore(permissions: PermissionCheckResult[]): number` that sums weights only for `granted` states, normalizes to 0–100 scale, and returns a deterministic integer, (c) export the weight table and scoring function along with a `PermissionDebtResult` interface containing `score`, `maxPossible`, and per-permission breakdown

Document scoring rules as JSDoc on the weight table and scoring function: explain that only 'granted' state contributes, 'denied'/'prompt'/'unsupported' contribute zero, describe the normalization formula (sum of granted weights / sum of all weights × 100, clamped to 0–100), and list each permission's weight with rationale (privacy-invasive permissions like camera, microphone, geolocation get higher weights than motion sensors)

Create `tests/us-022/permission-debt-score.test.ts` with Vitest covering: (a) all permissions granted → score 100, (b) no permissions granted (all denied/prompt/unsupported) → score 0, (c) only high-weight permissions granted → score reflects higher proportion, (d) only low-weight permissions granted → score reflects lower proportion, (e) mixed states across permissions → correct weighted sum, (f) denied permissions do not increase score, (g) unsupported permissions do not increase score, (h) prompt permissions do not increase score, (i) determinism check (same input twice → same output), (j) empty input array → score 0, (k) unknown permission names handled gracefully with a default weight
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all checks pass
testing_recommendations: Unit test: all 11 permissions granted → score equals exactly 100
Unit test: all permissions denied → score equals exactly 0
Unit test: all permissions unsupported → score equals exactly 0
Unit test: all permissions in prompt state → score equals exactly 0
Unit test: single high-weight permission (camera) granted, rest denied → score equals that permission's weight proportion
Unit test: single low-weight permission (accelerometer) granted, rest denied → score is lower than camera-only case
Unit test: mixed granted/denied/prompt/unsupported → only granted weights sum correctly
Unit test: determinism — calling function twice with identical input produces identical output
Unit test: empty permissions array → score 0 with no errors
Unit test: verify PermissionDebtResult includes per-permission breakdown with name, state, and weight
Unit test: verify score is always an integer between 0 and 100 inclusive (property-style check with multiple random inputs)
risks: Weight assignments are subjective — choosing appropriate risk weights for each permission requires a defensible rationale; document reasoning clearly so downstream consumers (US-023, US-027, US-029) can validate
If new permissions are added to the adapter in the future, the scoring module needs a sensible default weight to avoid silently ignoring them — plan includes a default weight fallback

The 0–100 normalization assumes a fixed set of permissions; if the adapter's permission list changes, the same set of granted permissions would yield a different score — mitigate by deriving max from the actual input array rather than a hardcoded constant
